title: About | Keir Mather MP
meta_description: About page for Keir Mather MP, Member of Parliament for Selby and Kippax
text_excerpt: --> About | Keir Mather MP top of page Keir Mather MP Phone Mail Phone keir.mather.mp@parliament.uk ​​ 01757 247 990 ​ 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW Home About Selby and Kippax North Yorkshire Council Leeds City Council Seeking support Local Campaigns SEND provision Road safety Improving mobile signal Tackling flooding Better buses Progress in Parliament Key Policy Statements News Contact Privacy Policy Standards About Keir Keir was elected as your Labour Member of Parliament for Selby and Kippax in the 2024 General Election. ​ Born and educated locally in Yorkshire, Keir moved on to to study History and Politics at Wadham College, Oxford before receiving a Master of Public Policy degree at University College, Oxford. ​ Keir was first elected in the Selby and Ainsty by-election in July 2023 and was returned as the Member of Parliament for the new Selby and Kippax constituency in the 2024 General Election, with a majority of 10,195. ​ ​ ​In Parliament, he has been fighting for the people of Selby on issues such as the cost of living crisis, SEND provision, rural crime, public transport and underperforming NHS services. Keir made his Maiden Speech in the House of Commons on 16th October 2023, during a debate on Early Years Provision . ​ Following the election of the Labour Government in July 2024, was appointed to HM’s Government as an Assistant Government Whip. In September 2025, Keir was appointed to serve as the Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State in the Department for Transport. ​ Keir lives in Selby and is a passionate supporter of the Rugby League team Hull KR. Stay updated on my work in the constituency and beyond Sign up for my newsletter © 2025 by Keir Mather MP. All rights reserved. Promoted by Keir Mather MP of 16 Park Street, Selby, YO8 4PW bottom of page
